content.title: Views & Rendering
content.description:
The rendering system transforms items into DOM trees using views. Understanding view lookup, the renderer API, and partial re-rendering is key to building custom views.

---

## What is a View?

A view is a code item with:
- `type`: [view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000)
- `content.for_type`: GUID of the type this view renders
- `content.code`: ES module exporting `render(item, api)`

### Minimal Example

```javascript
export async function render(item, api) {
  return api.createElement('div', { class: 'my-view' }, [
    item.content.title || 'Untitled'
  ]);
}
```

---

## View Lookup

When [`renderItem()`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=renderItem) is called, the system finds a view via [`resolveView()`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=resolveView):

### Preference Hierarchy

1. **Explicit**: `viewId` parameter passed to `renderItem()`
2. **Item preference**: `item.preferredView` field
3. **Type preference**: `typeItem.preferredView` field
4. **Type chain**: Walk up `extends` chain, find first view with matching `for_type`
5. **Fallback**: [kernel:default-view](item://aaaaaaaa-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) (JSON display)

### Setting Preferences

```javascript
// Item-level preference
await api.setPreferredView(itemId, viewId);

// Type-level preference (affects all items of type)
await api.setPreferredView(typeId, viewId);

// Contextual override (in specific container)
await api.setChildView(parentId, childId, viewId);
```

---

## The Renderer API

Views receive a comprehensive API object created by [`createRendererAPI()`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=createRendererAPI):

### DOM Creation

[`api.createElement()`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=createElement) creates DOM elements:

```javascript
api.createElement(tag, props, children)
```

Supports event handlers (`onclick`, etc.), `class`, `style`, and nested shorthand:

```javascript
api.createElement('div', { class: 'wrapper' }, [
  ['h1', {}, ['Title']],
  ['p', {}, ['Content']]
]);
```

### Item Operations

```javascript
const item = await api.get(itemId);          // Read item
const items = await api.query({ type: id }); // Query items
await api.set(item);                         // Save (no re-render)
await api.update(item);                      // Save and re-render
await api.create(item, addAsChild?);         // Create new item
await api.delete(itemId);                    // Delete item
```

### Rendering

```javascript
const dom = await api.renderItem(childId, viewId?, options?);
await api.rerenderItem(itemId);              // Partial re-render
await api.rerenderByView(viewId);            // Re-render all using view
```

### Navigation

```javascript
await api.navigate(itemId, params?);         // Navigate to item
api.siblingContainer?.addSibling(itemId);    // Open as sibling window
```

### View Discovery

```javascript
const views = await api.getViews(typeId);    // All views for type (see getViews region)
const view = await api.findView(typeId);     // Default view for type (see findView region)
const view = await api.getEffectiveView(itemId); // What would render
```

### Context

```javascript
const parentId = api.getParentId();          // Rendering parent
const viewId = api.getViewId();              // Current view ID
const config = api.getViewConfig();          // View config from parent
await api.updateViewConfig({ key: value });  // Persist view state
```

---

## Render Instance Registry

The [`RenderInstanceRegistry`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=RenderInstanceRegistry) in [kernel:rendering](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000) tracks every rendered DOM node:

### Indexes

- **By Item ID**: Find all places an item is displayed
- **By View ID**: Find all instances using a specific view
- **By Parent ID**: Find all attachments of a container

### Partial Re-rendering

Instead of full viewport re-render:

```javascript
await api.rerenderItem(itemId);    // Update specific item in place
await api.rerenderByView(viewId);  // Update when view code changes
await api.rerenderByType(typeId);  // Update when type preference changes
```

This preserves sibling state (scroll positions, form inputs).

---

## Cycle Detection

The rendering system tracks the render path via [`cycle-detection`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=cycle-detection). If an item appears twice:

```javascript
await api.renderItem(itemId, null, {
  onCycle: (item) => api.createElement('div', {}, [
    '↻ Already rendering ' + item.name
  ])
});
```

Without `onCycle`, an error is thrown with the full render path.

---

## View Configuration

Views can store persistent state in the parent's child entry:

### Reading Config

```javascript
const config = api.getViewConfig();
// For root items, reads from viewport-manager
// For nested items, reads from parent's attachments array
```

### Writing Config

```javascript
await api.updateViewConfig({
  sortOrder: 'name',
  collapsed: ['section-1']
});
```

Persisted in `parent.attachments[i].view` object.

---

## Sibling Container Protocol

Container views (like [spatial-canvas-view](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9)) provide a sibling container:

```javascript
// In a child view
if (api.siblingContainer) {
  link.onclick = () => api.siblingContainer.addSibling(targetId);
}
```

This opens the linked item as a new window/panel rather than navigating away.

---

## Creating a Custom View

### 1. Create the View Item

```javascript
await api.set({
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: 'my-custom-view',
  type: api.IDS.VIEW,
  content: {
    for_type: 'target-type-uuid',
    displayName: 'My Custom View',
    description: 'Renders items as...',
    code: `
export async function render(item, api) {
  const container = api.createElement('div', { class: 'my-view' });
  
  // Build your UI...
  const title = api.createElement('h1', {}, [item.content.title]);
  container.appendChild(title);
  
  return container;
}
    `
  }
});
```

### 2. Test It

Right-click an item of the target type → View As → select your view.

### 3. Set as Default (Optional)

In View Settings modal, set as default for type.

---

## Available Views

```query
const views = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.VIEW });
views.sort((a, b) => (a.name || '').localeCompare(b.name || ''));
return views
  .filter(v => v.content?.displayName)  // Only user-facing views
  .map(v => `- **${v.content.displayName}**: ${v.content.description?.split('\n')[0] || v.name}`)
  .join('\n');
```
